verses.text: Fear not in battle, for 'the LORD your God... goeth with you.' Courage. God fights for His people.
Fear not in battle, for 'the LORD your God... goeth with you.' Courage. God fights for His people.
Fear not in battle, for 'the LORD your God... goeth with you.' Courage. God fights for His people.
Fear not in battle, for 'the LORD your God... goeth with you.' Courage. God fights for His people.
The newly built, planted, and betrothed are sent home. Released. War spares the unready.
The newly built, planted, and betrothed are sent home. Released. War spares the unready.
The newly built, planted, and betrothed are sent home. Released. War spares the unready.
The faint-hearted are dismissed, lest they discourage others. Dismissed. Fear must not spread.
The faint-hearted are dismissed, lest they discourage others. Dismissed. Fear must not spread.
Offer peace first; if refused, besiege the city. Peace first. Mercy precedes war.
Offer peace first; if refused, besiege the city. Peace first. Mercy precedes war.
Offer peace first; if refused, besiege the city. Peace first. Mercy precedes war.
Distant cities, if they fight, are smitten, the spoil kept. Spoil. The far cities are dealt with.
Distant cities, if they fight, are smitten, the spoil kept. Spoil. The far cities are dealt with.
Distant cities, if they fight, are smitten, the spoil kept. Spoil. The far cities are dealt with.
The nations of the land are utterly destroyed, lest they teach sin. Devoted. Their sin would corrupt.
The nations of the land are utterly destroyed, lest they teach sin. Devoted. Their sin would corrupt.
The nations of the land are utterly destroyed, lest they teach sin. Devoted. Their sin would corrupt.
Destroy not the fruit trees in a siege. Spare trees. Even war has its mercy.
Destroy not the fruit trees in a siege. Spare trees. Even war has its mercy.
